body:
1. For example, inside an issue / PR (with agent tag), the user might sometimes want to talk to the agent but forget to tag the agent. 
2. We should infer the user intention: if that sounds like a followup question, then we should consider have sepo respond the user automatically. 

Conditions:
- this should be only activated when it has an agent tag 
- also this behavior can also be disabled by an customization variable (say AGENT_INTENTION_INFER = false) (or a better name?

@sepo-agent /answer

comments.body:
Yes, this fits the existing router, but the key change is earlier than triage: today the entrypoint only runs when the event JSON contains the handle, so an unmentioned follow-up comment never reaches `.agent/src/cli/extract-context.ts`.

I’d implement it as an opt-out implicit follow-up path:

- Treat “agent tag” as the fixed `agent` issue/PR label.
- Add repo variable `AGENT_IMPLICIT_FOLLOWUPS_ENABLED`; default enabled, set to `false` to disable.
- Only consider unmentioned follow-ups on issue/PR comments and PR reviews/comments, not arbitrary issue/PR body edits.
- For implicit candidates, only allow automatic `answer` or `ignore`; never auto-dispatch `implement`, `review`, `fix-pr`, or `orchestrate`.

Relevant current wiring:

- [`agent-entrypoint.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-entrypoint.yml#L48-L57) has the broad `contains(...AGENT_HANDLE...)` pre-filter that currently blocks unmentioned follow-ups.
- [`agent-router.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-router.yml#L161-L241) extracts context, runs dispatch triage when no explicit route is present, and resolves the route.
- [`extract-context.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/extract-context.ts#L146-L220) applies bot, association, live-mention, approval-command, and requested-route gates.
- [`context.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/context.ts#L266-L285) currently requires a live mention.
- [`triage.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/triage.ts#L58-L88) parses explicit slash commands after the mention.

Execution plan:

1. Loosen the entrypoint pre-filter so it also starts the router when `AGENT_IMPLICIT_FOLLOWUPS_ENABLED != 'false'` and the issue/PR payload has the `agent` label.
2. Pass `implicit_followups_enabled` into `agent-router.yml` and then into `extract-context`.
3. Extend context extraction to return `implicit_followup=true` when there is no live mention but the target is agent-labeled, authorized, non-bot, and on a supported follow-up event.
4. Add an internal `ignore` triage outcome for implicit follow-ups, or a small dedicated classifier, so casual comments like “thanks” do not post a response.
5. Force implicit follow-ups to `answer` only when the classifier says it is likely addressed to the agent, such as a follow-up question or request for clarification.
6. Add tests for explicit mentions unchanged, labeled follow-up question responds, unlabeled follow-up skips, variable-disabled skips, and non-question comments ignore.
7. Update the configuration list and architecture docs for the new variable and behavior.

If approved, I’d make this change with the `AGENT_IMPLICIT_FOLLOWUPS_ENABLED` name and the `agent` label as the activation tag.

title: Add smarter user intention detection
